summary: Frontend regression of the enhanced Breathe & Calm screen (voice/chime + age-appropriate exercises). All 4 requested test cases PASS: (1) Age-appropriate exercise sets render correctly — YOUNG (age 8) shows breathe-type-balloon + breathe-type-flower toggles plus the 'Gentle breaths made just for you! 🎈' note; OLDER (age 12) shows breathe-type-478 + breathe-type-box and no gentle note. (2) breathe-voice-toggle and breathe-chime-toggle both render, both ON by default, and toggle off/on while not running (label switches between 'Voice on'↔'Voice off' and 'Chime on'↔'Chime off'). (3) breathe-start triggers TTS pre-load — button text changes to 'Getting ready...' (Loader2 spinner) then the session runs, breathe-label cycles through 'Breathe In' steps with a visible countdown, breathe-cycles increments from 0 → 1 after a full Balloon cycle (in4/out4 ≈8s), and breathe-stop returns the page to the Begin state. (4) REGRESSION: activity is logged after ≥1 cycle (activities count for the child went 0 → 1 after stopping); the crisis-help-btn 'Get Help' is rendered on the Breathe screen. ZERO JS console errors, ZERO pageerrors, ZERO 4xx/5xx network responses observed across the entire flow including /api/tts pre-load and audio playback.
critical_code_review_comments: Breathe.js: age<=8 branch is correct but the seed-data default ('age 8' boundary) means the demo parent's seeded kids all hit YOUNG_EXERCISES. Consider whether age=8 should be young or older from a product POV — the boundary feels intentional (≤8 = gentle) so flagging only for awareness.

Breathe.js prepareVoice() awaits Promise.all of /api/tts calls inside start(); if one TTS call is slow it blocks the whole pre-load. Acceptable today (verified <3s end-to-end) but a per-label fallback (start session anyway after Nms, lazy-cache) would be more resilient on slow networks.
Breathe.js useEffect on [stepIdx, running] re-fires voice/chime on initial stepIdx=0 — works, but a deliberate first-step play is fine. No fix needed.
Minor: stop() posts the activity from the client without server-side cycle validation; a curious user could spam activities via DevTools. Server-side de-dup/threshold is a hardening item, not a bug.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (4/4: age-appropriate sets + toggles + run/stop session + activity log + crisis button)

seed_data_creation: Created one additional child profile 'OlderT7487' (age 12) under demo parent to exercise the OLDER_EXERCISES branch — the four pre-existing demo kids were all age 8 which is the YOUNG boundary. Can be removed via Parent Dashboard if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Demo parent now has 5 child profiles. age=8 ones (TestKid1c26, TestKidConf, ConfettiKid, ConfT94710) all map to YOUNG (balloon+flower). New OlderT7487 (id f14cab20-b493-4e54-8a61-727c59e1391c, age 12) maps to OLDER (478+box). Breathe screen fully verified: voice/chime toggles, TTS pre-load via /api/tts, label cycling, cycles counter, activity logging on stop after >=1 cycle, crisis-help-btn present. No console/network errors. Web Audio chime + HTMLAudio voice playback couldn't be auditioned in headless but no API errors during invocation.
